pulse: Note:  US markets were closed Monday, May 25 for Memorial Day. The data below reflects the close from Friday, May 22, 2026, the most recent trading session. Markets reopened Tuesday, May 26.

spotlights.name: Citigroup
spotlights.news:
Citi Wealth launches Citi Sky AI tool in partnership with Google Cloud and Google DeepMind
Q1 2026 EPS surpassed estimates by 17% at $3.31, with revenue up 17% YoY to $22.1B
Analysts raised price target to $142.50 citing improved valuation and earnings growth outlook
spotlights.verdict: BUY  Citigroup offers attractive valuation at 15.50x forward earnings with a $142.50 analyst target implying 13.8% upside from current levels. Strong Q1 2026 earnings (17% EPS beat, 17% revenue growth) demonstrate operational momentum and margin expansion, supported by AI-driven wealth management initiatives. Key risks include exposure to broader financial sector volatility and potential margin compression if interest rates decline faster than expected.

spotlights.name: Qorvo
spotlights.news:
Q4 FY2026 EPS $1.69 beat expectations (+37% surprise); shareholder vote approved Skyworks merger
High Performance Analog segment up 11.2% YoY; gross margins expanded 260bps driven by portfolio shift
FY2027 guidance: mid-single digit revenue decline as Android exposure reduced, HPA growth accelerates
spotlights.verdict: HOLD  Qorvo trades near analyst targets ($92-95 range) with attractive 13.7x trailing P/E supported by strong Q4 earnings surprise and HPA segment momentum. However, upcoming FY2027 revenue headwinds from Android de-prioritization and the pending Skyworks merger create near-term uncertainty; the stock has limited upside from current levels unless merger terms improve or guidance beats. Monitor execution on high-margin business pivots and competitive pressures in smartphone RF chips.

why_paras:
Broad-Based Gains Amid Geopolitical Optimism:  
. Despite modest moves in the major indices, 
President Donald Trump led a ceremony swearing in Kevin Warsh as chair of the Federal Reserve
, a significant development that ended weeks of uncertainty around monetary policy direction. The rally was driven primarily by improved sentiment around Middle East peace negotiations.

Iran Deal Hopes Drive Energy and Risk Appetite:  
Market strategists characterized the mood as "the everything rally," with traders focused more on the potential for peace in the Middle East than weekend risks
. 
Stocks jumped as oil prices and U.S. Treasury yields slid amid growing optimism that the conflict in the Middle East could soon be resolved
. 
SoftBank Group shares extended their scorching rally to a second day, rising over 11% Friday, and building on momentum after Nvidia's blockbuster earnings boosted AI-linked stocks, with the company's shares having closed 20% higher Thursday
. This relief in geopolitical tensions supported semiconductor and broader tech positions.

Sector Divergence and Strong Individual Performers:  
Tech is now the second-best performing sector of 2026 behind energy (which boasts a 34.5% return), while consumer staples, industrials, real estate, and materials are clustered together with year-to-date returns of between 9% and 11%
. Among individual names, QRVO surged 8.89% on strong earnings execution and analyst upgrades, reflecting the market's rotation toward profitable semiconductor plays benefiting from AI infrastructure buildout.
macro: Kevin Warsh's swearing in as Federal Reserve chair puts him in charge of navigating a tumultuous economy while managing a president with specific expectations on interest rates
—this creates both opportunity and uncertainty around whether the Fed maintains independence in future rate decisions.

econ.why_it_matters: Gauges manufacturing sector health and signals broader economic momentum amid persistent inflation concerns.
Federal Reserve guidance influences interest rate expectations critical to equity valuations and growth forecasts.
Labor market strength impacts consumer spending power and Fed policy decisions affecting growth and inflation dynamics.

stw.name: NVIDIA Corporation
stw.reason:
NVDA — NVIDIA Corporation  | Price: $215.33 |  BUY  | Horizon: 5-YEAR 
 Rationale: NVIDIA's dominant position in AI infrastructure with next-generation products targeting $91B guidance supports long-term compounding despite near-term margin pressures. Diversifying AI customer base and massive addressable market create exceptional 5-year return potential for patient capital.

stw.name: Micron Technology
stw.reason:
MU — Micron Technology  | Price: $745.55 |  BUY  | Horizon: 5-YEAR 
 Rationale: Micron's explosive growth in AI memory demand, with advanced 1-alpha DRAM production ramping in Virginia and record free cash flow generation, positions it as a multi-year compounding opportunity. Supply constraints and $120B+ addressable market through 2026 underpin sustained upside.

stw.name: Microsoft Corporation
stw.reason:
MSFT — Microsoft Corporation  | Price: $418.57 |  HOLD  | Horizon: 1-YEAR 
 Rationale: Azure's AI infrastructure strength and enterprise cloud momentum provide steady growth, but premium valuation at 24.9x P/E and revenue inflation concerns warrant a measured 1-year outlook. Monitor quarterly guidance and AI monetization metrics before increasing exposure in a rich market.

stw.name: Meta Platforms
stw.reason:
META — Meta Platforms  | Price: $610.26 |  SELL  | Horizon: EXIT NOW 
 Rationale: Meta's 9.5% monthly decline and downtrend since August 2025 alongside weaker momentum versus S&P 500 signals deteriorating relative strength; recent 10% workforce reduction adds execution risk. Exit positions and redeploy to better-positioned AI beneficiaries with clearer monetization paths.

stw.name: Advanced Micro Devices
stw.reason:
AMD — Advanced Micro Devices  | Price: $467.51 |  BUY  | Horizon: 5-YEAR 
 Rationale: AMD's 213% year-to-date rally driven by MI450 ramp and $120B server CPU opportunity growing at 35%+ annually offers multi-year architectural tailwinds. Signed hyperscaler commitments and inflecting CPU franchise provide visibility despite current valuation premium to capitalize on data center share gains.
